title: Clinical Examination — General Anesthesia: Standard Intravenous Induction (Non–Rapid Sequence)

items.text: Don PPE and observe universal precautions
Verbalize the anesthesia time-out prior to induction

Apply standard ASA monitors and obtain baseline vital signs
Pulse oximeter · blood pressure · ECG (BIS/CVP PRN); assess baseline vitals

Confirm readiness for induction
Machine checked · suction on · monitors on · airway equipment available · IV access · induction drugs available

Position (“sniffing”) and pre-oxygenate
100% FiO₂ for 3 minutes at normal tidal breaths; verbalize ETO₂ > 90%

Verbalize and administer induction medications
Appropriate selection · correct doses · correct sequence

Confirm ability to mask-ventilate before the neuromuscular blocking agent
Assess LOC / eyelash reflex, then confirm positive-pressure ventilation before the paralytic

Administer neuromuscular blocking agent and manage onset
Verbalize agent and dose · demonstrate PPV while awaiting blockade · re-assess vital signs

Prepare to intubate
Confirm unconsciousness/apnea · eye protection · confirm readiness (TOF 0/4) · continue SaO₂ monitoring

Laryngoscopy
Open mouth (jaw thrust / scissor) · insert blade avoiding teeth & soft tissue · verbalize anatomy (pharynx, epiglottis, glottis, cords)

Endotracheal intubation
Pass ETT through glottis under direct vision · remove blade & stylet · inflate cuff · connect breathing circuit

Confirm endotracheal tube placement
Continuous ETCO₂ waveform · symmetric chest rise · bilateral breath sounds · absent epigastric sounds

Secure airway and initiate maintenance
If unable to intubate, mask-ventilate · turn on inhaled agent · secure ETT · set ventilator (mode, Vt, RR, fresh gas, FiO₂, bag→vent)

Closeout and overall competency
Re-assess vitals & adjust agent · verbalize readiness for surgery · appropriate rescue if unable to intubate · intubation in < 3 attempts
